user_query:
I’m the hospital CEO working closely with our CIO to accelerate safe digital transformation. Please design a one-quarter micro-curriculum that advances our strategic priorities while respecting staff time limits and reusing our existing trainings where possible.

Inputs you must use:
- input/strategic_priorities.md (our goals and constraints)
- input/roles.yaml (roles and max training hours per quarter)
- input/existing_training.csv (current internal modules)

External research requirement (Internet):
- Perform search engine queries to identify at least two authoritative competency frameworks or guidance documents relevant to healthcare cybersecurity, digital health adoption, or health IT safety from any of these organizations: US Department of Health and Human Services (including ONC or OCR), NIST, AHRQ, HIMSS, or The Joint Commission. Use official publications only. Extract the key competency categories or domains you will align to.
- Do not include direct URLs in deliverables. Identify each source by organization and document title (and year/version if available). Keep a plain-text log of the search queries you used and the final sources you selected.

Deliverables and constraints:
1) output/frameworks_summary.json
   - JSON array of objects, each with: {
       "source_title": string,
       "publishing_body": string (one of the organizations listed above),
       "year_or_version": string (if available, else "unknown"),
       "competency_categories": array of strings (the main domain/category names you extracted),
       "citation_note": string (one-sentence description)
     }
   - Include at least two distinct sources from the allowed organizations.

2) output/curriculum_plan.csv
   - Columns: module_id,title,audience_roles,hours,delivery_mode,source_alignment,new_or_existing,priority_mapping
   - audience_roles: semicolon-separated subset of roles from input/roles.yaml or "All"
   - hours: numeric, >0
   - delivery_mode: one of {"self-paced","live-virtual","in-person"}
   - source_alignment: semicolon-separated references to the competency categories and source titles from frameworks_summary.json (e.g., "NIST <category>; HHS <category>")
   - new_or_existing: if reusing an internal module, put the existing module_id from input/existing_training.csv; otherwise "New"
   - priority_mapping: semicolon-separated subset drawn from the priorities in input/strategic_priorities.md
   - Requirements:
     • Cover each strategic priority from input/strategic_priorities.md with at least one module.
     • Reuse existing modules when their topic_tags and audience_roles reasonably cover a needed competency and audience; propose new modules only for gaps.
     • For each role in input/roles.yaml, the sum of hours assigned to modules that include that role (or "All") must not exceed that role’s max_hours_per_quarter.
     • Include at least one module that maps to cybersecurity resilience, one to EHR usability, one to interoperability/data sharing, one to AI governance/literacy, and one to incident response readiness.

3) output/validation.json
   - JSON object with:
     {
       "hours_by_role": {"<role>": total_hours},
       "within_budget": {"<role>": true|false},
       "frameworks_used_count": integer,
       "frameworks_orgs": ["<org1>","<org2>",...],
       "new_module_count": integer,
       "existing_module_reused_count": integer,
       "covers_all_priorities": true|false
     }
   - Compute hours_by_role by summing hours of all modules in curriculum_plan.csv where audience_roles includes that role or "All". Determine budgets from input/roles.yaml. frameworks_orgs and frameworks_used_count should reflect output/frameworks_summary.json. covers_all_priorities should be true only if every priority from input/strategic_priorities.md appears in curriculum_plan.csv’s priority_mapping.

4) output/executive_memo.md
   - Write a concise executive memo to me and the CIO with the following sections (use these exact headings):
     • Overview
     • External frameworks consulted
     • Curriculum plan summary
     • Hours by role vs. budget
     • Risks and next steps
   - In “External frameworks consulted,” list each source by publishing body and document title (no URLs). In “Hours by role vs. budget,” include the totals and indicate any role that would exceed limits (should be none).

5) output/search_log.txt
   - Plain text capturing at least three distinct search engine queries you executed and the titles/organizations of the sources you ultimately selected.

Notes:
- Build the plan for one quarter only.
- Keep the total number of new modules reasonable (aim for 5 or fewer) and favor reuse of existing modules in input/existing_training.csv.
- Make sure every module in output/curriculum_plan.csv clearly maps to at least one competency category from output/frameworks_summary.json and at least one strategic priority from input/strategic_priorities.md.

Success criteria:
- All five output files are present with the specified structures and fields.
- Per-role hours in output/validation.json are within the limits from input/roles.yaml and covers_all_priorities is true.
- frameworks_summary.json includes at least two authoritative sources from the allowed organizations, and those sources are reflected in curriculum_plan.csv and the executive memo.
- The memo uses the exact section headings specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
